testcase.name: test_fault_injection_short_to_ground_state
testcase.failure:
fault_injection = <conftest.FaultInjectionController object at 0xffff88c00c20>
def test_fault_injection_short_to_ground_state(fault_injection): """ HIL Test: Verify fault injection controller correctly applies SHORT_TO_GROUND. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_005
Scenario: 1. Inject a SHORT_TO_GROUND fault on the CAN_H line. 2. Assert the controller reports the active fault type. 3. Clear the fault and verify state returns to NONE.
On real HIL: the relay board would physically short CAN_H to GND, causing bus-off errors or dominant bit stuffing violations. """ > from tests.conftest import FaultInjectionController E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:166: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_fault_injection_open_circuit_no_response
testcase.failure:
can_bus = <can.interfaces.virtual.VirtualBus object at 0xffff88b63cb0> fault_injection = <conftest.FaultInjectionController object at 0xffff88c08b90> ecu_reset = None
def test_fault_injection_open_circuit_no_response(can_bus, fault_injection, ecu_reset): """ HIL Test: Verify ECU silence when brake sensor line has an open circuit fault. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_006
Scenario: 1. Inject OPEN_CIRCUIT on BRAKE_SENSOR_A (simulated: virtual bus ignores this). 2. Send a brake pedal message — in virtual mode the ECU still responds. 3. Assert fault controller confirms the fault was applied.
On real HIL: an open circuit on the sensor wire would prevent the ECU from receiving a valid pedal signal, resulting in a safe-state (0% pedal) response. The test assertion would then check for the safe-state response value instead. """ > from tests.conftest import FaultInjectionController E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:201: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_fault_injection_teardown_always_clears
testcase.failure:
fault_injection = <conftest.FaultInjectionController object at 0xffff88c09950>
def test_fault_injection_teardown_always_clears(fault_injection): """ HIL Test: Verify fault is cleared by fixture teardown even if test raises. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_007
Safety Requirement (ISO 26262): A fault left active on the bench after a test failure could damage downstream tests or real hardware. The fixture teardown MUST clear all faults unconditionally.
Scenario: 1. Inject a fault. 2. Assert fault is active mid-test. 3. Test passes — teardown will call controller.clear("ALL"). The fixture teardown in conftest guarantees clear() runs even on exception. """ > from tests.conftest import FaultInjectionController E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:243: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_power_supply_nominal_voltage_on_start
testcase.failure:
power_supply = <conftest.BenchPowerSupply object at 0xffff88c01d30>
def test_power_supply_nominal_voltage_on_start(power_supply): """ HIL Test: Verify power supply starts at 12V nominal and output is enabled. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_008
Scenario: The power_supply fixture initialises the PSU at NOMINAL_VOLTAGE (12.0V) with output enabled. Assert the state before any test manipulation. """ > from tests.conftest import BenchPowerSupply E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:269: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_power_supply_low_voltage_ecu_still_responds
testcase.failure:
can_bus = <can.interfaces.virtual.VirtualBus object at 0xffff88b63cb0> power_supply = <conftest.BenchPowerSupply object at 0xffff88c0a5d0> ecu_reset = None
def test_power_supply_low_voltage_ecu_still_responds(can_bus, power_supply, ecu_reset): """ HIL Test: Verify ECU still processes brake pedal at 9V (discharged battery). Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_009
Scenario: 1. Drop supply to 9V (minimum operating voltage per ISO 16750-2). 2. Send a brake pedal message. 3. Assert ECU still responds (it must operate down to 9V). 4. Restore 12V nominal.
On real HIL: PSU SCPI command "VOLT 9.00" is issued over VISA. ECU supply rail drops; current draw typically increases slightly. """ > from tests.conftest import BenchPowerSupply E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:292: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_power_supply_overvoltage_clamped
testcase.failure:
power_supply = <conftest.BenchPowerSupply object at 0xffff88c0aad0>
def test_power_supply_overvoltage_clamped(power_supply): """ HIL Test: Verify PSU rejects voltages above MAX_VOLTAGE (16V) as a safety guard. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_010
Scenario: Attempt to set PSU to 20V (above MAX_VOLTAGE limit of 16V). The BenchPowerSupply.set_voltage() must raise ValueError to protect hardware.
On real HIL: the PSU itself also has hardware OVP (Over Voltage Protection); this test validates the software safety layer catches the error first. """ > from tests.conftest import BenchPowerSupply E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:330: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_low_voltage_abs_activation_logged
testcase.failure:
can_bus = <can.interfaces.virtual.VirtualBus object at 0xffff88b63cb0> power_supply = <conftest.BenchPowerSupply object at 0xffff88bed5b0> can_logger = 'capture_test_low_voltage_abs_activation_logged.asc' ecu_reset = None
def test_low_voltage_abs_activation_logged( can_bus, power_supply, can_logger, ecu_reset ): """ HIL Test: Verify ABS activates correctly at low supply voltage, with CAN log. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_015
Fixtures used: power_supply + can_logger + can_bus + ecu_reset
Scenario: 1. Drop supply to 9V (battery drain simulation). 2. Set speed > 100 km/h AND pedal > 80% → ABS must activate. 3. Log all CAN traffic during the test. 4. Assert ABS bit (Bit 0) is set in status frame (0x400). 5. Restore nominal voltage.
This is a typical compound HIL test: electrical stress + functional verification + full traffic capture for traceability. """ > from tests.conftest import BenchPowerSupply E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:491: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_fault_injection_with_uds_session_and_logging
testcase.failure:
can_bus = <can.interfaces.virtual.VirtualBus object at 0xffff88b63cb0> fault_injection = <conftest.FaultInjectionController object at 0xffff88bee2c0> uds_session = 'EXTENDED' can_logger = 'capture_test_fault_injection_with_uds_session_and_logging.asc' ecu_reset = None
def test_fault_injection_with_uds_session_and_logging( can_bus, fault_injection, uds_session, can_logger, ecu_reset ): """ HIL Test: Verify ECU behaviour under wire fault with active UDS session, fully logged. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_016
Fixtures used: fault_injection + uds_session + can_logger + can_bus + ecu_reset
Scenario: 1. Open a UDS Extended Session (e.g. to enable DTC storage). 2. Inject a SHORT_TO_VBATT fault on the brake sensor line. 3. Send a brake pedal message — ECU must still not crash (safe state). 4. Clear the fault. 5. Verify CAN traffic was captured throughout.
This type of test validates fault tolerance during diagnostic activity — a critical ISO 26262 ASIL-B scenario for brake ECUs. """ > from tests.conftest import FaultInjectionController E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:542: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_power_cycle_restores_abs_state
testcase.failure:
can_bus = <can.interfaces.virtual.VirtualBus object at 0xffff88b63cb0> power_supply = <conftest.BenchPowerSupply object at 0xffff88bf9010> signal_monitor = <conftest.SignalMonitor object at 0xffff88bef100> ecu_reset = None
def test_power_cycle_restores_abs_state( can_bus, power_supply, signal_monitor, ecu_reset ): """ HIL Test: Verify ABS state is cleared after ignition cycle and DAQ shows nominal voltage. Polarion: SWE_REQ_HIL_017
Fixtures used: power_supply + signal_monitor + can_bus + ecu_reset
Scenario: 1. Activate ABS (speed=120km/h, pedal=90%). 2. Power cycle the ECU (simulates ignition off/on). 3. After boot, verify ABS is no longer active (state was reset). 4. Sample ECU supply voltage via DAQ and confirm it is back to nominal. """ > from tests.conftest import BenchPowerSupply E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
functional_tests/test_hil_brake_controller.py:585: ModuleNotFoundError
